projects.title: Unit 4 Project — Rates & Percents in Action
projects.desc: Capstone: apply rates and measurement conversions to a real-world challenge.

projects.title: Ratio Table Fuel Mixer
projects.desc: Use ratio tables and conversion factors to mix and scale measured amounts (6.RP.3d).

projects.title: Recipe Factory — Scale the Ratio
projects.desc: Scale recipe quantities and convert measurement units using ratios.

title: Convert Measurement Units
contentObjective: I can convert measurement units using ratios and conversion factors.
languageObjective: I can explain my conversion using the words conversion factor, unit, equivalent, and ratio.

turnAndTalk.question: The cabinet blueprint uses feet but the parts catalog uses inches. Why do you have to convert, and which is the bigger unit, a foot or an inch?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A foot is bigger than an inch, so I must convert to compare or combine the measurements.

stems.en: I have to convert because the blueprint uses ___ but the catalog uses ___.
stems.es: Tengo que convertir porque el plano usa ___ pero el catálogo usa ___.
Un/Una ___ es más grande que un/una ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain a foot is the larger customary unit (12 inches) and that mixed units must be converted to the same unit before working with them.
turnAndTalk.extend: Predict: when you change feet into inches, will the number get bigger or smaller? Explain using the size of each unit.
extendStems: Switching to a smaller unit means ___.

turnAndTalk.question: How did you use a conversion factor like '1 ft = 12 in' to fill in the ratio table, and how did you decide whether to multiply or divide?
turnAndTalk.kernel: I multiply when going to a smaller unit and divide when going to a bigger unit.

stems.en: ___ equals ___ because the conversion factor is ___.
stems.es: ___ es igual a ___ porque el factor de conversión es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students use the conversion factor as a ratio, multiply going to smaller units (5 ft x 12 = 60 in) and divide going to larger units (48 in / 12 = 4 ft).
turnAndTalk.extend: Feet-to-inches uses multiply but inches-to-feet uses divide, even though both use '12.' Explain why the same conversion factor leads to opposite operations.
extendStems: I multiply when ___ but divide when ___.
The same factor works both ways because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A doorway is 80 inches tall and a new cabinet is listed as 7 feet. Your friend says it won't fit because 7 feet is 82 inches. Will it fit, and where is the error?
turnAndTalk.kernel: 7 feet is 84 inches, which is taller than the 80-inch doorway, so it will not fit.

stems.en: Since ___ inches is ___ than 80 inches, the cabinet ___ fit.
stems.es: Como ___ pulgadas es ___ que 80 pulgadas, el gabinete ___ caber.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students convert 7 ft to 84 in (not 82), compare 84 > 80, and conclude the cabinet will not fit; they catch the friend's arithmetic error.
turnAndTalk.extend: How many inches would you need to trim off the cabinet to make it fit, and is that even possible for a real cabinet? Justify your reasoning.
extendStems: You would need to remove ___ inches because ___.
That is or is not realistic because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Convert Measurement Units, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Multiply when changing to a smaller unit, and divide when changing to a bigger unit.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.RP.3d — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: A ratio that helps you change one unit into another.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una razón que te ayuda a cambiar una unidad por otra.
vocabulary.visual: 1 foot = 12 inches, so the factor is 12
vocabulary.cloze: A ratio equal to 1 that relates two units, like 12 inches / 1 foot, is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A standard amount used to measure, like an inch or a liter.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una cantidad estándar para medir, como una pulgada o un litro.
vocabulary.cloze: A standard amount used to measure, like a foot or a gram, is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: Having the same value, just in different units.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Tener el mismo valor, solo en unidades distintas.
vocabulary.cloze: Two measurements that name the same amount, like 1 foot and 12 inches, are ___.

vocabulary.definition: A way to compare two amounts.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una manera de comparar dos cantidades.
vocabulary.cloze: A comparison of two quantities is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: The U.S. way of measuring, like inches, feet, and pounds.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La forma de medir de EE. UU., como pulgadas, pies y libras.
vocabulary.visual: 12 inches = 1 foot, 3 feet = 1 yard, 16 ounces = 1 pound
vocabulary.cloze: Units like inches, feet, and pounds used mainly in the U.S. are ___.

examples.why: A foot is a customary unit of length.
A meter is a metric unit.

vocabulary.definition: A way of measuring by 10s, like meters, liters, and grams.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una forma de medir de 10 en 10, como metros, litros y gramos.
vocabulary.cloze: Units like meters, grams, and liters based on tens are ___.

examples.why: A gram is a metric unit of mass.
A pound is a customary unit.

launch.narrative: You're building a brand-new arcade cabinet! The blueprints use feet, but the parts catalog lists measurements in inches. The wiring instructions use yards, and the weight limits are in pounds — but the shipping labels use ounces. You need to convert between units to make sure everything fits and ships correctly.
launch.contextImage: Arcade cabinet blueprint with measurements in mixed units — feet, inches, and yards
noticePrompts: Why might the blueprint and the parts catalog use different units?
How many inches are in 1 foot? How many feet in 1 yard?
wonderPrompts: How do you decide whether to multiply or divide when converting?
Can you use a ratio table to help convert units?

conceptIntro.heading: How do I change one measurement unit into another?
conceptIntro.intro: A unit is a standard amount used to measure, like a foot or an inch. A conversion factor is a ratio that relates two units, like 1 foot = 12 inches.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Multiply when changing to a smaller unit, and divide when changing to a bigger unit.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: The cabinet is 5 feet tall. I want to know how many inches that is.
My conversion factor is 1 foot = 12 inches.
Inches are smaller than feet, so I multiply: 5 × 12 = 60.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: A wire is 4 yards long. The factor is 1 yard = 3 feet.
Feet are smaller than yards, so do we multiply or divide? (Multiply)

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the table, use the conversion factor to fill in each converted measurement.
Decide each time: are you going to a smaller unit (multiply) or a bigger unit (divide)?

explore.instructions: Use conversion factors to complete the ratio table. Convert each measurement to the target unit.

discourse.prompt: How do you decide whether to multiply or divide when converting units?
discourse.sentenceFrame: When converting from a ___ unit to a ___ unit, I ___ by the conversion factor because ___.

optional.stem: Leg 1: The joystick rod is 3 feet long, but the parts catalog lists it in inches. How many inches is that? (1 ft = 12 in)
optional.explanation: Inches are smaller than feet, so multiply: 3 × 12 = 36 inches.

optional.stem: Leg 2: A power cable on the cabinet plan measures 2.5 kilometers across the whole arcade network. How many meters is that? (1 km = 1,000 m)
optional.explanation: Meters are smaller than kilometers, so multiply: 2.5 × 1,000 = 2,500 meters.

optional.stem: Leg 3: The coin box weighs 5 pounds, but the shipping label needs the weight in ounces. How many ounces is that? (1 lb = 16 oz)
optional.explanation: Ounces are smaller than pounds, so multiply: 5 × 16 = 80 ounces.

optional.instructions: Leg 4: Match each part's measurement to its equivalent value to pass it to the next station.
optional.label: Equivalent Measurements

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Multiply or Divide?

workedExample.label: Convert 4 feet of side panel to inches

optional.correctWork: Inches are smaller than feet, so you multiply (not divide): 4 × 12 = 48 inches. The answer should be larger, not smaller, when changing to a smaller unit.
hints: You are going from a larger unit (feet) to a smaller unit (inches). Should the number get bigger or smaller?
Changing to a smaller unit means more of them, so multiply: 4 × 12 = 48 inches.

optional.stem: Final Leg: The finished cabinet ships in a crate holding 8 quarts of packing gel, but the supplier sells gel by the gallon. How many gallons is that? (4 qt = 1 gal)
optional.explanation: Gallons are bigger than quarts, so divide: 8 ÷ 4 = 2 gallons.

approaching.stem: How many inches are in 7 feet?
How many feet are in 3 yards?
How many ounces are in 4 pounds?

approaching.instructions: Complete the conversion table using the given conversion factors.

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Convert Measurement Units.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Multiply when changing to a smaller unit, and divide when changing to a bigger unit.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Multiply when changing to a smaller unit, and divide when changing to a bigger unit.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Multiply when changing to a smaller unit, and divide when changing to a bigger unit.

onLevel.instructions: Match each measurement to its equivalent value.

onLevel.stem: A shelf is 30 inches long. How many feet and inches is that?

onLevel.instructions: An arcade cabinet has parts measured in different units. Convert each to the target unit.
When converting, do you multiply or divide? Sort each conversion by the correct operation.

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Convert Measurement Units.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Multiply when changing to a smaller unit, and divide when changing to a bigger unit.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Multiply when changing to a smaller unit, and divide when changing to a bigger unit.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Multiply when changing to a smaller unit, and divide when changing to a bigger unit.

extending.title: Find the Conversion Error
extending.correctWork: Since 3 feet = 1 yard, you divide by 3 (not multiply). 6 ÷ 3 = 2 yards.
hints: You're going from a smaller unit (feet) to a larger unit (yards). Should you multiply or divide?
Converting to a larger unit gives a smaller number. 6 feet ÷ 3 = 2 yards.

extending.prompt: A recipe says you need 5 feet of ribbon, but the store only sells ribbon in inches. Another recipe says you need 2 yards of fabric, but your ruler measures in feet. Convert both measurements and explain the steps you used. Which conversion factor did you use each time?
extending.sentenceFrame: 5 feet = ___ inches because ___. 2 yards = ___ feet because ___. The conversion factors I used were ___.

extending.label: Place these lengths on a number line in inches: A = 1 foot, B = 1.5 feet, C = 2 feet, D = 1 yard

optionalActivity.name: Measurement Conversion Relay
optionalActivity.intro: Race your arcade build down the assembly line — clear each leg by converting the part to the unit the next station needs.

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Convert Measurement Units is skipping the key idea: "Multiply when changing to a smaller unit, and divide when changing to a bigger unit." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: The arcade is ordering a new game cabinet. The doorway is 80 inches tall. The cabinet is listed as 7 feet. Your friend says it won't fit because 7 feet is 82 inches.
connect.promptQuestion: Will the cabinet fit through the doorway? Show your conversion to prove it.
connect.prompt: 7 feet = 7 × ___ = ___ inches. Since ___ inches is ___ than 80 inches, the cabinet ___ fit through the doorway.

exitTicket.stem: Which is the correct conversion of 5 yards to inches?
exitTicket.explanation: 5 yards × 3 = 15 feet. 15 feet × 12 = 180 inches. Or: 5 × 36 = 180 inches.

printables.desc: Students convert customary and metric units up and down a ladder using conversion factors.
